topic: Two friends both apply for the same job
raw_output:
[story 1]

Liam stared at the email, his thumb hovering over the 'open application' button. He had spent weeks polishing his resume, tailoring his cover letter to the precise language of the marketing manager role at Stellar Corp. Then, a notification popped up: 'Application received. We will be in touch shortly.' He clicked, bracing himself for the usual silence, the slow crawl of bureaucratic assessment. But when he scrolled down, a second email appeared, addressed to him, mentioning an interview scheduled for Tuesday afternoon. A sudden, sharp intake of breath escaped his chest. He reread the details, his eyes darting back and forth across the screen, trying to reconcile the reality of the situation with the calm certainty he’d felt moments before. His hands, resting on the cool wooden desk, began to tremble slightly.

[story 2]

"No way," Maya whispered, the sound barely audible over the hum of the coffee machine. She leaned back in her chair, her jaw slackening as she stared at the printed confirmation slip. It was for the Senior Developer position at Innovatech. She had submitted hers three days ago, meticulously proofreading every line of code sample. Now, tucked neatly beside her own paperwork, was a confirmation for *her* application, also for that very role. Her fingers, which had been tapping impatiently on the keyboard, froze mid-air. She slowly lifted her gaze from the paper, her brow furrowing deeply. A slow, disbelieving smile stretched across her face, quickly replaced by a look of utter bewilderment as she reread the identical job description one last time.

[story 3]

"Did you see this?" Ben exclaimed, his voice pitched higher than usual. He held up his tablet, his knuckles white where he gripped the device. The screen displayed the results from the online portal. He had applied for the position at Zenith Solutions, the one he’d been aiming for since graduation. Beneath his name, there was a secondary notification, an acknowledgement of another submission for the exact same opening. Ben blinked rapidly, a strange, vacant look washing over his features. He dropped the tablet onto the sofa cushion with a soft thud. He stood up abruptly, pacing three quick steps before stopping, running a hand through his already messy hair. He let out a low, unsteady laugh that caught in his throat.
